title: Jonathan Binder

I am a PhD student in the Seed Science and Technology Research Group at Royal Holloway, University of London. I am studying the effects of soil microbiological communities on the longevity of blackgrass (Alopecurus myosuroides) seeds in the soil seedbank. My studies are funded by the London Interdisciplinary Biosciences Consortium Doctoral Training Partnership (LIDo) with Syngenta AG supporting as an industry partner.


The Pennsylvania State University | State College, PA, USA
MSc in Agronomy | Mar 2019
Vassar College | Poughkeepsie, NY, USA
BA in Environmental Studies | May 2014

BA in Environmental Studies | May 2014

title: Projects
text:
Microorganisms can play an important role in plant health, but seed-associated microorganisms have often been overlooked. My research intends to determine how microorganisms affect Alopecurus myosuroides seed persistence in the soil seed bank.






Alopecurus myosuroides at flowering. Credit: Krzysztof Ziarnek, Kenraiz, CC BY-SA 4.0, via Wikimedia Commons







Alopecurus myosuroides spikelets (commonly seeds). Credit: Steve Hurst @ USDA-NRCS PLANTS Database, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

The Pennsylvania State University | MSc in Agronomy | 2019

저는 로열 홀러웨이 런던대학교에 있는 종자 과학 기술 연구 그룹에 속한 박사생입니다. 미생물 커뮤니티 쥐꼬리뚝새풀 (Alopecurus myosuroides) 토양시드뱅크내 종자 지속성에 끼치는 영향을 공부합니다. 농약회사 신젠다 제 연구를 지원합니다.
